routing_rule: All drafts land in Ryan's Outlook drafts folder. Owner reviews, fills TO, edits, sends manually.
send_policy: Drafts-only. No auto-send. Human approval required per JetPakt guardrail.

drafts.subject: A quiet observation about Casa Bonita — and a free preview
drafts.body:
Hi there,

I'm Ryan — I run JetPakt, a small Denver-based practice that reads public restaurant reviews carefully and turns what guests are already saying online into something you can act on. I'm reaching out to Casa Bonita specifically because I've been going through your public reviews this week and there's one pattern worth surfacing.

The theme I keep seeing cluster — entirely in public, on Google and Yelp — is the way service fees and automatic charges are surfaced on the bill. Here's one verbatim quote from a recent review that captures it cleanly:

    "Was never told about the 15% automatic service charge until the bill arrived."

This is the kind of pattern that tends to draw both regulatory attention (Colorado's HB25-1090 took effect January 1) and the occasional private-action suit, so it's worth getting in front of before the review volume grows. I'm not a lawyer and this isn't legal advice — just a heads-up from someone who reads these reviews for a living.

I'd like to send you a free one-page preview — drawn entirely from your public reviews — showing the rating trend, the two most-cited complaint themes, and one verbatim quote that captures each. No commitment, no sales call, no CRM follow-up. If it's useful, a full Scan (the complete 8-pillar audit with draft response templates) is $49 and delivered in about 48 hours.

If you'd like the preview, just reply with 'yes' and I'll have it to you within two business days. If not, I won't follow up.

A note on how I work, so nothing's a surprise: every finding I send you is anchored to a verbatim public quote — I never name individual staff, never invent a claim, and I never post or email anything on your behalf. Every response template is a draft that lands in your inbox for your approval.

More detail at https://poetic-melba-f04633.netlify.app if useful.

— Ryan B.
JetPakt · Denver
gojetpakt.us@outlook.com
Gojetpakt.com

drafts.subject: Something Denver guests are saying about El Tapatio Mexican Restaurant — Arvada
drafts.body:
Hi there,

I'm Ryan — I run JetPakt, a small Denver-based practice that reads public restaurant reviews carefully and turns what guests are already saying online into something you can act on. I'm reaching out to El Tapatio Mexican Restaurant — Arvada specifically because I've been going through your public reviews this week and there's one pattern worth surfacing.

The theme I keep seeing cluster — entirely in public, on Google and Yelp — is a broader quality-trajectory concern that I flag for legal review (I never repeat unverified illness allegations as fact). Here's one verbatim quote from a recent review that captures it cleanly:

    "Ten years ago it was one of my favorite places — the quality has completely slipped."

I'd like to send you a free one-page preview — drawn entirely from your public reviews — showing the rating trend, the two most-cited complaint themes, and one verbatim quote that captures each.
